q1.question: What do I need to prepare before using the AI video workflows?
q1.answer: At minimum, you need a script, a few reference images, or a base clip. For text-to-video templates, clear scene descriptions and timing notes help the model plan shots. For image-to-video, upload sharp images at 1080p or higher with the key subject clearly visible. Avoid heavy watermarks, motion blur, or cropped-off subjects, as these reduce quality.

q2.question: How long do generations take and how are credits used?
q2.answer: Most templates generate a 5-10 second clip within a few minutes, but heavier video models can take up to around 10 minutes depending on settings and load. Higher-end models like Sora or Veo 3.x consume more credits per run than lighter models. A workflow may call several models (for example, script -> video -> VFX -> music), and each step uses credits once it runs. You can test a shorter segment or a more affordable model first, then switch to higher quality for the final output.

q3.question: How much control do I have over motion, framing, and style?
q3.answer: You control the general story, pacing, and look, but not every single frame. Use structured prompts, shot lists, and reference images to guide composition and movement. For more predictable results, keep prompts consistent across runs, lock in key reference frames, and iterate on the same workflow instead of starting from scratch. Extremely precise choreography or camera paths may still require manual editing after generation.

q4.question: Can I combine multiple templates or models in one project?
q4.answer: Yes. OpenCreator is a workflow editor: you can start from a script-to-film template, feed the results into lip-sync, VFX, or background-music nodes, and then assemble everything into a final cut. You can also swap individual nodes to try different video models without rebuilding the whole flow, which makes it easy to A/B test creative variations while keeping the same structure.

q5.question: Can I use AI-generated videos commercially and for client work?
q5.answer: In general, videos generated in OpenCreator can be used commercially, including for ads and social campaigns. However, you are responsible for respecting each model provider's terms, local regulations, and platform policies. Do not upload assets you do not have rights to (logos, stock footage, faces) and review final outputs for compliance before delivering them to clients.
